metaTitle: The Boeing Atmosphere Global Documentation Hub - Components - Data Tables

header.title: Data table
header.description: Data tables display sets of data across rows and columns

items: Data tables display information in a grid-like format of rows and columns. They organize information in a way that’s easy to scan, so that users can look for patterns and insights.
Data tables can contain interactive components (such as buttons, checkboxes or menus)
Data tables can contain non-interactive elements (such as badges)
Data tables can contain tools to query and manipulate data

data.resource: Figma: Atmosphere Foundations and Components Library

sections.text:
Baseline elements

Column header names describe the type of content displayed in each column. Each row contains data related to a single entity.

  1. Column header
  2. Row cell

sections.textBefore:
Interactive elements

Data tables can include interactive elements.
sections.textAfter:
1. Checkboxes
  2. Sorting arrows
  3. Inline menus
  4. Buttons (tonal) or icon buttons (standard)

    5. Controls that manipulate how table data is displayed, such as filters or pagination, should be placed directly above or below a table in an action bar. More information about the action bar can be found in the Tables pattern section.

sections.textBefore:
Content modifiers

Table cells can contain different types of content, including text, icons, checkboxes, tags, action buttons, and profiles.

sections.text:
Usage

Data tables display information in a grid-like format of rows and columns. They organize information in a way that’s easy to scan so that users can look for patterns and develop insights from data.

Data tables can contain:

  • Interactive components (such as buttons, checkboxes or menus)
  • Non-interactive elements (such as badges)
  • Tools to query and manipulate data

Data tables should be:

  • Organized: Information should be organized in a meaningful way, such as hierarchical or alphabetical
  • Interactive: Data tables should allow user interaction so that the data and the view is customizable and interactive
  • Intuitive: Data tables should be easy to use, with a logical structure that makes content easy to understand

More information about the data tables can be found under Patterns - Tables.

sections.textAfter:
Column states (hover)

When the user hovers over a column header:

  • A tooltip can display the full column name (if it’s truncated) or a more detailed description of the column
  • If sorting is enabled, an arrow icon can appear next to the column header.

Text

Column header text uses a medium highlight weight font to differentiate from row text. Text that is longer than the column width is truncated with an ellipsis. On hover, a tooltip shows the full name.

Sorting

To show users that they can sort the information in a table, apply default sorting.Place a downward or upward arrow icon next to the title of the column header to indicate which column is sorted by default.

sections.textBefore: To reverse the sort order of a column, a user taps the header name or arrow icon. Upon sorting, the arrow icon reverses direction.

sections.textAfter:
Scrolling

Horizontal and vertical scrolling is available on data tables. Action bars, header rows, and columns can be fixed so that they do not leave the viewport even when scrolled.

sections.text:
Use cases

Users should be able to:

  • Understand the hierarchy of the information clearly
  • Navigate within the table using assistive tech 
  • Select or deselect rows
  • Trigger actions and customize the data display
  • Maintain visibility of the action bar, header row, or columns while scrolling (optional)

Keyboard navigation

Users should be able to navigate to and activate the elements inside the Data table using these keyboard keys:

data.actions: Focus lands on first available element
For focused row: Opens row content (optional)
For closed menus: Opens menu or submenu.
For open menus: Selects list item
For checkboxes: Toggles a focused checkbox between selected and unselected
For focused row: Moves focus to the next row
For open menus: Moves focus to the next item

sections.text: When a user navigates to a table using the tab key, the active indicator appears on the first interactive element, providing feedback to the user that it is selected. The user is then able to tab to additional interactive elements until all available items have been visited within the data table.

Cursor

Interaction with the data table will be dictated by the action elements it contains (sorting, filtering, multi select, icon buttons, action buttons, etc.). The user will be able to execute its function by clicking on them.

The cursor will change according to each element’s behavior.

Responsiveness

Data tables should be used for XL, L and M window sizes. Data tables can be swapped for list items or cards for XS or S screen sizes.

Densities

The data table will be reduced in height for condensed versions. All of these changes can be visually seen in the Measurements section.

data.layer: Checkbox or icon button target size
